body: When public inspection shows a forecast task with no missingness, reliable unchanged period evidence, a localized medium level-excursion signal, low local robust-z peak, low estimated level offset, flat imputation/clipping probes, negative denoising probes, and flat level-correction probes, do not use global denoising, imputation, or clipping as repair candidates. Supply at most one behavior-distinct PROGRAM candidate as a narrow contrast to identity: repair_level_shift over only the estimated excursion region using the public estimated_region_start_fraction, estimated_region_end_fraction, and estimated_level_offset bindings. Keep identity as the control candidate. During selection, treat flat level-correction probe response as non-disqualifying structural evidence for this narrow local contrast, not as a reason to prefer identity, when verification confirms the program is effect-distinct, its modified fraction is within max_modified_fraction, and the effective view is unchanged outside the estimated region. Choose the supplied repair_level_shift program under those verified conditions; otherwise explicitly select identity. Record the inspected region, candidate identity, modified-fraction guard, outside-scope preservation check, and that flat level-correction probes were not used as a veto in this scoped structural case.
